subject: Statistics
title: News Consumption as Bayesian Updating
description: One-minute skill: News Consumption as Bayesian Updating.

hook.text: A headline says: 'Coffee causes cancer!' Should you stop drinking coffee? A Bayesian would say: not yet.

buildup.text: Your prior: decades of coffee research show mixed results. One study doesn't override thousands.

discovery.text: Check the likelihood: was the study large, well-designed, and replicated? If not, it barely moves the needle.

twist.text: Most people update too much on single headlines. Bayesian thinking provides emotional insulation against noise.

climax.text: Consuming news like a Bayesian means updating gradually, not panicking at every new headline.

punchline.text: One headline isn't enough to change a strong prior.

quiz.question: How should a Bayesian thinker respond to a shocking news headline?
options: Consider it as one data point that slightly updates a strong prior — don't overreact
Immediately change behavior based on the headline
Ignore all news because it's unreliable
